narrative.headline: 2022 Subaru Impreza: power train and visibility lead a small set of complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed just 12 complaints with NHTSA. Power train and visibility/wiper tie for the top spot at 25% each (3 reports apiece), followed by steering at 17%. None of the reported issues involve a crash, fire, injury, or death. With so few complaints overall, no single problem stands out as widespread.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Tied for most reported: 3 reports (25%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Watch for a loss of drive power or warning lights coming on.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: Tied for most reported: 3 reports (25%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Complaints describe windshield cracking. Inspect the glass closely.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 2 reports (17%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners note overly sensitive steering. Pay attention to wheel feel on a test drive.

issues.sample: The vehicle's rear brake lines (both left rear and right rear) had ballooning present. The issue was initially identified by an independent mechanic during brake pad and rotor replacement on all four wheels. The ballooning was subsequently confirmed during an inspection by a Suba
issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: A single report (8%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. One owner found ballooning rear brake lines. A rare complaint.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: A single report (8%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. One owner describes hesitation when accelerating. A rare complaint.
